trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Meet another Nobel laureate & our distinguished visiting journalist
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG 10-06-25

urls.page_title: Journalist offers master class on op-eds, narrative storytelling
urls.page_summary: Journalist and biographer Sam Tanenhaus will share his writing expertise with the Cornell community in a master class, “Op-Eds and Narrative Storytelling, on Oct. 8 in Lewis Auditorium, Goldwin Smith Hall.
urls.page_title: Behavioral economics: Born at Cornell, embraced by policymakers
urls.page_summary: Economists and psychologists work together to understand how human behavior impacts people's decision-making in the marketplace.
urls.page_title: Nobel-winning behavioral economist Richard Thaler to speak Oct. 17
urls.page_summary: Thaler won the Nobel Prize in 2017 for work done in the 1980s at Cornell. He is now the Charles R. Walgreen Distinguished Service Professor of Behavioral Science and Economics at the University of Chicago.
urls.page_title: Physicist: After 33 billon years, universe ‘will end in a big crunch’ | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: After expanding to its peak size about 11 billion years from now, the universe will begin to contract – snapping back like a rubber band to a single point at the end, according to a Cornell physicist.
urls.page_title: Big Red Teaching Days | Center for Teaching Innovation
Caring for a Baby Makes the World Seem More Dangerous, Says New Study - Newsweek
urls.page_summary: The findings indicate that infants’ looks and movements influence how adults process sensory information.
urls.page_title: The Man Who Built a Movement: How William F. Buckley Invented Modern Conservatism - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Sam Tanenhaus, journalist, historian, biographer, and this fall's Zubrow Distinguished Visiting Journalist (DVJ) in the College of Arts and Sciences, is the author of “Buckley: The Life and the Revolution that Changed America.” He'll discuss Buc...
urls.page_title: Faculty respond to request to share progressive teaching ideas, study shows
urls.page_summary: The team found a significant uptick in the number of articles published after 2013 that focused on core concepts and competencies suggested in a seminal report.
